summary: Matched the item function `it_8026D9A0` by adding its C implementation in `src/melee/it/it_266F.c` and exposing the needed `it_80276214` prototype in `src/melee/it/it_2725.h`. The function updates item collision state via `it_80276214`, calls `mpColl_800477E0` on `item->x378_itemColl`, copies the collision position back to `item->pos`, conditionally stores the floor index in `item->xC30`, and returns the collision result. The PR body only says it resolves issue #1066; there were no review comments.
what_changed: Created `src/melee/it/it_266F.c` with includes for platform, item inlines/types, item header declarations, map collision, and HSD gobj support.
Used `GET_ITEM(gobj)` to obtain the `Item*` and took `CollData* coll_data = &item->x378_itemColl`.
Assigned `item->pos = coll_data->x4_vec` after the map collision call.
If the collision result is true, assigned `item->xC30 = coll_data->floor.index`.
Updated `it_2725.h` to add the prototype `bool it_80276214(Item_GObj*)`.
Adjusted nearby prototypes in `it_2725.h` to omit some argument names while preserving types.
smart_moves: Introduced the local `CollData* coll_data` alias to keep field accesses concise and likely closer to the compiler's expected register allocation.
Preserved the collision update order: prepare/update item collision with `it_80276214`, run `mpColl_800477E0`, then copy the resulting collision vector to `item->pos`.
Only updates `item->xC30` when the collision test returns true, matching the conditional behavior shown in the diff.
Added a small unnamed padding local `u8 _[4];`, suggesting a stack-shape or matching workaround used to reproduce the original codegen.
Added the missing header declaration for `it_80276214` instead of relying on an implicit declaration.
decomp_lessons: For item collision helpers, keep item position synchronization explicit: collision data's `x4_vec` can be copied back to `item->pos` after map collision processing.
When matching small item functions, stack-shaping locals such as an unused `u8 _[4]` may be required; document or preserve them when they are necessary for matching.
Header updates can be required even for a small function match when the implementation depends on an existing but undeclared sibling routine.
Use existing item access conventions such as `Item_GObj*`, `Item*`, and `GET_ITEM(gobj)` rather than open-coding gobj user data access.
Conditionally propagating collision metadata such as `coll_data->floor.index` should follow the return value of the map collision routine.
naming_conventions: Unmatched or address-named item functions retain the `it_8026D9A0` and `it_80276214` style.
Item object parameters use `Item_GObj* gobj` in implementations and many prototypes.
Item data is conventionally retrieved with `Item* item = GET_ITEM(gobj)`.
Collision data field naming follows existing struct names such as `x378_itemColl`, `x4_vec`, and `floor.index`.
The header keeps address comments such as `/* 276214 */` before item function prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a scoped block around a dummy local, temporary result, assignments, and return, likely to influence stack lifetime or code generation.
Inserted `u8 _[4];` before the `bool temp` local, which is likely a matching tactic for stack layout or instruction scheduling.
Stored the `mpColl_800477E0` result in a local `bool temp` and returned it after side effects rather than returning directly.
Used a local pointer to `item->x378_itemColl` so the same `CollData*` is passed to `mpColl_800477E0` and reused for field reads.
follow_up_queries: Find other item functions that call `mpColl_800477E0` and then copy `coll_data->x4_vec` to `item->pos`.
Search for other matches using dummy locals like `u8 _[4]` for stack-shape fixes.
Inspect issue #1066 for any additional context on why `it_8026D9A0` was targeted.
Check whether `item->xC30` has since been renamed or typed more specifically than a raw field offset.
Look for later PRs that rename `it_80276214` or document its role in item collision setup.
